payload.text: Vamos adicionar novamente de volta, a opção de seleção de cor individual para cada coisa. Texto, Highlight, fundo e etc. Tudo de acordo com as cores da palleta. Não altere e nem mude mais nada na plataforma, apenas adicione isso

**Re-evaluating Color Choices**

I'm reconsidering the initial approach to color selection for the front-end elements. My goal is to reintroduce granular control, allowing specific color selections for text, highlights, and backgrounds, guided by the brand palette. The challenge lies in ensuring that these choices are implemented in `index.tsx` in a way that respects consistency and scalability.


**Implementing Color Selectors**

I'm now integrating individual color selectors for text, highlight, background, accent, and card background within the "DESIGN" tab of `App.tsx`. These selectors will offer a selection of brand palette colors with an option for custom color picking, balancing minimal updates with visual consistency. The focus is to make these updates functional, user-friendly, and in the right location.


**Adding Color Customization**

I'm focusing on "Manual Color Overrides" within the "DESIGN" tab. I'm adding `ColorSelector` components for properties like background, text, accent, and highlights. The interface will feature palette suggestions from the brand alongside custom color input, enhancing user control while maintaining brand consistency. I'm refining how the selected colors are applied and integrated.


**Refining Color Controls**

I'm now incorporating detailed UI enhancements for color controls. Within the "DESIGN" tab of `App.tsx`, I'm creating a "Cores do Slide" section, mapping color properties and integrating with `currentSlide.options` for read/write functionality. The interface now features a grid with color pickers and suggested swatches derived from the active brand palette, aligning with the user's need for granular control. I'm focusing on labels for each color control to make it user friendly: Background, Text, Accent, Highlight (HL), Highlight Text, Card Background, and Card Text.


**Finalizing Color Integration**

I'm now completing the implementation of the detailed color controls within the "DESIGN" tab. I'm focusing on the integration of color pickers and palette swatches for various elements, ensuring state updates via `updateSlideProperty`. I've added color selectors: Background, Text, Accent, Highlight BG, Highlight Text, Card BG, and Card Text. I'm verifying the read/write functionality with `slide.options` to confirm real-time updates within `SlideCanvas.tsx` for visual confirmation.

entries.description: Added individual color selection controls for Slide Background, Text, Accent, Highlight BG, Highlight Text, Card BG, and Card Text within the DESIGN tab. These controls provide quick-pick swatches based on the active brand palette as well as custom color pickers.

**Refining UI Aesthetics**

I'm focused on aesthetics now, specifically spacing. I'm aiming for a slightly increased space throughout the application, as the existing spacing feels cramped. Accessibility is, of course, a core consideration.


**Addressing Color Visibility**

My current concern is color visibility within the UI, particularly the background colors of controls and text. They are too light, making them difficult to discern. I'm exploring a color picker UI redesign to make it more obvious; I'm considering using a "rainbow" or colored border to differentiate these controls from static elements, and avoid the prior confusion. I'm also still committed to maintaining the core spacing adjustments.


**Improving Color Accessibility**

I'm now prioritizing color accessibility and contrast issues. Specifically, I'm darkening backgrounds, like the side panel and inputs, for better readability. I'll also brighten text labels. Furthermore, I'll enhance the color picker's visual representation with a rainbow ring around the color preview to provide a clear indication of color selection and focus.


**Enhancing UI Visuals**

I'm now implementing the design adjustments. I've updated the `ColorPropertyControl` component to feature a distinctive color picker look, using a gradient background to highlight its interactive nature. Also, I've increased padding in the side panel and expanded the spacing between controls for improved visual clarity. I've also adjusted label color contrast to ensure better readability. I'm focusing solely on these UI elements, leaving the rest untouched. The changes are applied inside `App.tsx`.

entries.description: Improved UI aesthetics: increased spacing between sections, enhanced label contrast for better readability, and added a distinct rainbow visual cue to color pickers to differentiate them from static color swatches.

entries.description: Restored the "FX" tab content in the sidebar, providing controls for Lighting Intensity, Noise (Grain), Vignette, and Clarity. The UI follows the same high-contrast and spaced-out design language as the updated Design tab.

Com certeza. Vamos restaurar essas funcionalidades essenciais com um design ainda mais refinado e funcional, garantindo que a edição de conteúdo seja intuitiva e que o bloco de branding (USER) tenha seu seletor de foto devidamente visível.

Especificação das atualizações:

  1. Floating Text Toolbar (Editor de Texto):

    • Adição de um campo textarea no topo da barra flutuante. Isso permite que, ao selecionar um texto no canvas, você possa editá-lo diretamente na janela que aparece, além de ajustar o estilo.
    • A janela agora exibe o conteúdo atual do bloco automaticamente ao abrir.

  2. USER Block (Branding) na aba BLOCKS:

    • Reintrodução da miniatura de avatar clicável.
    • Implementação de um input de arquivo invisível sobre o avatar para facilitar o upload da foto de perfil.
    • Manutenção dos campos de Nome e @Handle em uma disposição limpa ao lado da foto.

  3. Estética e Usabilidade:

    • A barra flutuante mantém o estilo "glassmorphism" escuro com bordas sutis.
    • O seletor de cor na barra flutuante também recebeu o visual de arco-íris para manter a consistência com a aba de Design.

entries.description: Restored text editing capability in the floating toolbar and added the avatar selection/upload feature to the USER block in the BLOCKS tab.

entries.description: Adicionado o atributo data-block-type="USER" para permitir que o editor flutuante identifique e edite corretamente o conteúdo do bloco de usuário (nome e @handle).

Restaurada a edição de texto do bloco USER no sidebar, adicionado controle para exibir/esconder o nome (hideName) e corrigida a edição de texto tanto no sidebar quanto na barra flutuante, garantindo que os campos sempre tenham valor definido.
